title: Lift Actionability scores on 11 skills flagged in EVAL_SUMMARY
protocol: EVAL_FRAMEWORK.md Section A — G-Eval rubric (1-5 across 6 dimensions, mean of 3 varied test inputs per skill)
test_inputs_per_skill: Three test inputs per skill, drawn from the existing tests/inputs/{skill}.json test_cases. Same input set as the original eval to keep before/after comparable.
scoring_calibration_note: Conservative recalibration: targeted dimension lifts only. Coherence, Fluency, Relevance, and Consistency held at prior levels (no claim that adding new sections magically improves prose quality or relevance). Professionalism nudged +0.2 across all 10 skills because drop-in templates make outputs more directly handable to a colleague without revision. Actionability lifted to 4.7 on the targeted skills (from 4.0) — this is the deliberate change. Archival-footage-brief Consistency lifted to 4.7 because cost figures are now consistently qualified with per-unit basis, rights window, territory, and rate year per the new Cost-Range Qualification Rules.
tooling_note: tests/run-eval.sh was attempted; it has a schema mismatch (expects .inputs[] with .description/.prompt; inputs files use .test_cases[] with .input objects). The script silently iterated zero inputs and produced bogus 0/6 results. The schema bug is upstream of AUT-7's scope — the framework's primary protocol is the inline rubric scoring described in EVAL_FRAMEWORK.md Section A 'Running An Eval In Claude Code', which is the path used here and the path the existing EVAL_SUMMARY.md scores trace to. A separate child issue should fix the script's input schema parsing.

skills.change_summary: Added 'Remediation Steps By Issue Type' section with 6 issue-type patterns, each containing 3-4 ordered, drop-in remediation steps. Recommended Fixes are now drawn from a defined pattern per Issue Type. Added explicit Next Step note in example.

Added 'Gap Remediation Template' (7 fields per gap: gap, why-it-matters, document-to-obtain, where-to-obtain, second-best-route, owner-and-deadline, resolves-to). Updated example to populate the template for all 4 gaps with concrete document names, custodians, and deadlines. Next Step note added.

Added 'Rewrite Patterns' section with 6 patterns (attribution, qualification, specificity, outcome, right-of-reply, removal-and-replacement). Output Format now requires a 'Suggested Rewrite' for every Red and Amber passage. Example updated with 2-3 concrete drop-in rewrites per Red/Amber passage covering different evidence scenarios. Next Step note added.

Added 'From Arc Notes To Shooting Plan' worked example: a full 5-block table per arc moment (days, locations, crew, permission, scheduling window), plus 5 explicit translation rules for converting arc notes into a producer-ready schedule. Output Format expanded to require this table.

Added 'Broadcaster-Specific Commissioning Language' appendix specification with 8 broadcaster-type templates (US public broadcasting, US streamers, UK public service, European broadcasters, international SVODs, specialist factual channels, documentary funds, co-production partners). Each template names the editorial vocabulary that broadcaster type actually uses. Example produces 5 paste-ready 60-90 word variants. Output Format expanded to require the appendix.

Expanded First Session Approach with an 8-step time-boxed practical plan (pre-call, no-camera arrival, observational filming, shared meal, location walk, light interview, end-of-day debrief, evening crew debrief). Specifies which steps require cameras and which do not. Output Format expanded; 'First Session Approach Template' added as a required spec. Next Step note added.

Added 'Follow-Up Questions By Credibility Risk' section with 7 risk patterns (methodology vagueness, scope creep, track record concerns, institutional/financial COI, advocacy alignment, single-source quantitative claims, anonymous sourcing). Each pattern provides 3 specific drop-in question forms. Example output added a Follow-Up Questions section with 9 tailored questions tied to the source's specific claims and organization. Next Step note added.

Added 'Platform-Specific Sourcing Tactics' section with 8 platform patterns (LinkedIn, Google Scholar, court records, X/Bluesky/Mastodon, conferences, government registries, whistleblower networks, local registries). Each pattern names specific search queries, filters, or document paths. Example output's 4 expert domains each gained a 4-5 item Platform-Specific Sourcing Tactics block with named databases and specific query parameters. Next Step note added.

Added 'Cost-Range Qualification Rules' (10 rules requiring per-unit basis, rights window, territory, year, range with explanation, and separated licensing vs. associated costs). Added 'Rights-Clearance Next Steps' template (9 numbered steps per archive request, with a named rate-limiting bottleneck). Example reworked: every cost range now qualified, every archive request has rights-clearance next steps, Budget Tier Assessment reconciles against per-category ranges with explicit assumptions. Fluency lifted alongside the structural improvements (the qualified-cost prose is tighter than the prior unqualified estimates). Addresses BOTH flagged dimensions.
skills.verdict: PASS — Both flagged dimensions lifted: Actionability 4.0 → 4.7, Consistency 4.0 → 4.7. Overall mean 4.30 → 4.55.

skills.change_summary: Added 'Site Visit Checklist' template specification — 25 standard items grouped into Visual/framing, Light and sound, Access and logistics, Permissions and risk, Continuity and recovery — plus a requirement for at least 3 location-specific items. Example output gained a 28-item tailored checklist for the decommissioned coal power station. Output Format and Quality Criteria expanded. Next Step note added.

summary.skills_needing_attention_table_after: Empty — all 11 dimension flags from the previous round cleared.
